title: The absolute tolerance parameter should be provided when using math.isclose() to compare values to 0
description: Ensures that an absolute tolerance is provided when using math.isclose() to compare values close to 0. This prevents numerical inaccuracies due to floating-point representation.
message: The absolute tolerance parameter should be provided when using math.isclose() to compare values close to 0.
impact: Numerical inaccuracies due to floating-point representation can lead to incorrect comparisons and logic errors.
recommendation: Provide an absolute tolerance when using math.isclose() to compare values close to 0.

noncompliant.code: if abs(x) < 0.001: print('x is close to 0')
noncompliant.description: Does not provide an absolute tolerance.

compliant.code: if abs(x - 0.001) < 0.0001: print('x is close to 0')

why_is_this_an_issue: Numerical inaccuracies due to floating-point representation can lead to incorrect comparisons and logic errors.
how_to_fix: Provide an absolute tolerance when using math.isclose() to compare values close to 0.

metadata.title: The absolute tolerance parameter should be provided when using math.isclose() to compare values to 0
